content.full_text:
WALLACE EDGERTON 805 Coronado Avenue, Long Beach CA 90804
August 12, 1992
State of California Fair Political Practices Commission 
FDFC
Post Office Box 807 Attention: Blanca Breeze 
AU$ 1 I ,ggz
Sacramento, California 9581 2
i'
Dear Ms. Breeze
I am requesting an opinion in ivritir-rg regarding are telephone conversation of 817192
about the definition of surplus campaign funds.
My term ended on July 20th (midnight) 1992. Surplus funds would begin at the end of
the post election reporcing period covering my term in of{ice ("my defeat") which
ended July 2fth.
Since the reporting period covering July 20th has not occurred, I wish to roll my
campaign funds into another re-election campaign fund. This campaign fund would be
treated as a campaign fund not as surplus funds.
This issue seems to be made clear in the California Election Code, Chapter 5, 12400.
use of surplus campaign funds: "...or at the end of the post election reporting period
following the defeat of the candidate... whichever occurs last..." the operative term
would appear to be following the "defeat" - which must mean last day in office-
otherwise one could be confused between the primary and general election, either one
perhaps being the cause of the defeat. Further confusion could arise because of
different dates for primaries-and lastly,the absurdity of referring to a campaign repoftint
period as surplus funds when in fact it covered a portion of incumbency. The latter
would occur with me if the reporting period were used which ended June 31, 1992,
leaving another 20 days of incumbency unaccounted for.
I also note that under #87204, (Leaving Office) that my disclosure statement is not due
until August 20, 1992,30 days after my incumbency.
Your kind and prompt attention to this matter would be gratefully appreciated-since my
re-election committee is concerned that I not proceed without the written
confirmation of the conclusion we reached during our telephone conversation of
August 7th.

sections.question: I am requesting an opinion in writing regarding are telephone conversation of 8/7/92 about the definition of surplus campaign funds. My term ended on July 20th (midnight) 1992. Surplus funds would begin at the end of the post election reporting period covering my term in office ("my defeat") which ended July 20th. Since the reporting period covering July 20th has not occurred, I wish to roll my campaign funds into another re-election campaign fund. This campaign fund would be treated as a campaign fund not as surplus funds.
sections.question_synthetic: Can a candidate whose term ended on July 20, 1992, roll campaign funds into a re-election campaign fund rather than treating them as surplus funds, based on the timing of post-election reporting periods under California Election Code Section 12400?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a candidate to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present. The requestor references a prior telephone conversation with Ms. Blanca Breeze on August 7, 1992, and seeks written confirmation. Document contains legal citations to California Election Code sections 12400 and 87204.

Since the reporting period covering July 20th has not occurred, I wish to roll my campaign funds into another re-election campaign fund. This campaign fund would be treated as a campaign fund not as surplus funds.

embedding.summary: Wallace Edgerton requests written FPPC advice regarding the treatment of campaign funds following the end of his term in office on July 20, 1992, seeking clarification on whether funds can be rolled into a re-election campaign rather than treated as surplus funds.
